jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase that defines Homer's character, instantly recognizable and economical

jokes.text: Open the door, Maude! I don't have time for the secret knock!
jokes.punchline: Ned still follows protocols even in panic
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned Flanders character comedy - his politeness even in crisis situations

jokes.text: A large bearlike animal, most likely a bear... has wandered down from the hills in search of food or, perhaps, employment.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the bear might be job hunting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist news reporting - the casual suggestion that a bear might be seeking employment is perfectly delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Looks like bad news for the... Impson family.
jokes.setup: Reporter trying to read family name on mailbox
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Simpson' as 'Impson'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation gag - functional but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: Everyone's gonna be just fine, as long as I've got enough beers.
jokes.punchline: Homer's only concern is having enough beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - prioritizing beer over family safety is perfectly in character

jokes.text: If I'm gonna be trapped inside the house... I gotta go out and buy some beer.
jokes.punchline: Solution is to go outside to buy more beer
jokes.explanation: Homer's circular logic at its finest - defeats the purpose of being trapped for safety

jokes.text: Now, to drop gracefully through the windshield.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to get out of car
jokes.punchline: Describing an inherently ungraceful action as graceful
jokes.explanation: Nice contradiction between intention and reality - sets up the physical comedy to follow

jokes.text: Sweet dreams, Smokey.
jokes.punchline: Gentle farewell to unconscious bear
jokes.explanation: Homer's unexpected tenderness toward the bear that terrorized the town

jokes.text: Book 'em, Lou. One count of being a bear.
jokes.punchline: Treating being a bear as a criminal offense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum absurdity - treating animal instincts as criminal behavior

jokes.text: And one count of being an accessory to being a bear.
jokes.punchline: Even more ridiculous additional charge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the absurdity - 'accessory to being a bear' is perfectly illogical legal terminology

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, it was horrible! We were trapped in the house all afternoon. And, well, we had to drink toilet water.
jokes.punchline: Extreme exaggeration of minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to dramatize minor inconveniences - toilet water after one afternoon

jokes.text: It's like a freakin' Country Bear Jamboroo around here!
jokes.punchline: Reference to Disney attraction mixed with profanity
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference with Moe's characteristic gruffness - solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: This is the first and only bear I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about constant bear attacks
jokes.punchline: Ned points out there was only one bear
jokes.explanation: Ned as voice of reason deflating Homer's hysteria with simple facts

jokes.text: We're here! We're queer! We don't want any more bears!
jokes.punchline: Adapting gay rights chant for bear complaint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of protest chants - the rhyme works perfectly while being completely absurd

jokes.text: Oh, I heard it at the mustache parade they have every year.
jokes.setup: Asked where he learned the chant
jokes.punchline: References fictional mustache parade
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual mention of absurd local event as if it's normal

jokes.text: They swiped my 'pic-a-nic' basket.
jokes.punchline: Yogi Bear reference with characteristic pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Straightforward cartoon reference - functional but predictable

jokes.text: The Bear Patrol must be working like a charm.
jokes.punchline: Crediting expensive patrol for solving one-bear 'problem'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the rock/tiger analogy perfectly - Homer's post-hoc reasoning

jokes.text: That's specious reasoning, Dad. - Thank you, honey.
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it as compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Lisa's intelligence vs Homer's obliviousness in minimal words

jokes.text: Thank you, honey.
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Homer's obliviousness turning criticism into praise

jokes.text: By your logic, I could claim this rock keeps tigers away... But I don't see any tigers around here, do you?
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates correlation vs causation with absurd example
jokes.explanation: Brilliant demonstration of logical fallacy that became a cultural reference - perfectly structured educational comedy

jokes.text: Lisa, I want to buy your rock.
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and wants to buy it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing his daughter's lesson about logical fallacies

jokes.text: Bear Patrol Tax $5.00. What? This is an outrage! It's the biggest tax increase in history!
jokes.setup: Homer sees small tax on paycheck
jokes.punchline: Calls $5 the biggest tax increase in history
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overreaction to minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Actually, Dad, it's the smallest tax increase in history.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's factual correction deflates his outrage
jokes.explanation: Lisa as voice of reason deflating Homer's drama with simple facts

jokes.text: Let the bears pay the bear tax. I pay the Homer tax.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks 'homeowner tax' is named after him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer egocentric thinking - assumes tax is named after him rather than homeowners

jokes.text: Are these morons getting dumber, or just louder? - Dumber, sir.
jokes.punchline: Aide's matter-of-fact confirmation they're getting dumber
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Dumber, sir.
jokes.punchline: Aide gives definitive answer
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - aide's matter-of-fact response to rhetorical question

jokes.text: 'Immigants.' I knew it was them! Even when it was the bears, I knew it was them.
jokes.setup: Mayor blaming immigrants for bear problem
jokes.punchline: Moe's mispronunciation and circular blame logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of scapegoating mentality - Moe's mispronunciation and illogical blame assignment

jokes.text: 'Immigants.' I knew it was them!
jokes.punchline: Moe mispronounces 'immigrants' and claims he always knew
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Moe's mispronunciation and instant scapegoating

jokes.text: Hey, German boy! Go back to 'Germania.'
jokes.punchline: Making up fake country name 'Germania'
jokes.explanation: Shows ignorance of townspeople but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You want to pick on immigrants, then pick on Willie!
jokes.punchline: Willie volunteers himself as better target
jokes.explanation: Willie's self-deprecating offer - character-appropriate but straightforward

jokes.text: Willie, please! The children want to pick on someone their own size.
jokes.setup: Willie volunteering to be picked on instead of student
jokes.punchline: Skinner treating bullying as legitimate if age-appropriate
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Skinner's bureaucratic approach to prejudice and bullying

jokes.text: Our schools are so jam-packed with immigrants, kids like Bart have lost the will to learn.
jokes.punchline: Blaming immigrants for Bart's academic disinterest
jokes.explanation: Homer finding absurd excuse for Bart's pre-existing academic problems

jokes.text: There's no denying it, Sis.
jokes.setup: Homer's absurd claim about immigrants affecting Bart's learning
jokes.punchline: Bart going along with obviously false excuse
jokes.explanation: Bart opportunistically agreeing with excuse for his poor performance

jokes.text: I forget which one exactly.
jokes.setup: Grandpa about to tell family immigration story
jokes.punchline: Doesn't remember which country they're from
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson - starting grand story but forgetting key details

jokes.text: sliced bread having been invented the previous winter.
jokes.setup: Grandpa saying America was greatest thing since sliced bread
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions sliced bread was invented recently
jokes.explanation: Clever deconstruction of common phrase by making the timeline literal and recent

jokes.text: We had to move out once we'd filled the hat with garbage. The end.
jokes.setup: Grandpa's immigration story about living in America
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic ending about garbage-filled hat
jokes.explanation: Typical Grandpa story structure - builds up then deflates with mundane detail

jokes.text: You? I don't believe it.
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked reaction despite obvious accent/appearance
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to the obvious - sets up character growth

jokes.text: You? I don't believe it.
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked reaction despite obvious accent/background
jokes.explanation: Simple obliviousness joke but not particularly clever

jokes.text: There was so much I wanted to see and to do and to have done to me.
jokes.setup: Apu expressing regret about leaving America
jokes.punchline: The oddly phrased 'have done to me' ending
jokes.explanation: Subtle character humor - Apu's formal speech pattern creating awkward implication

jokes.text: I got so swept up in the scapegoating and fun of Proposition 24
jokes.setup: Homer realizing his mistake about Proposition 24
jokes.punchline: Describing scapegoating as 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest admission about enjoying mob mentality

jokes.text: You know what really 'aggravateses' me is them 'immigants.'
jokes.punchline: Multiple mispronunciations in single sentence
jokes.explanation: Barney's characteristic mispronunciations showing his lack of education

jokes.text: Yeah, those are exactly my 'sentimonies.'
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'sentiments' as 'sentimonies'
jokes.explanation: Moe's malapropism - functional character comedy but predictable

jokes.text: Calcutta Technical Institute.
jokes.setup: Apu mentions graduating from Cal Tech
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's Calcutta Technical Institute, not California
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - audience assumes prestigious Cal Tech until clarification

jokes.text: top student in my graduating class of seven million
jokes.punchline: Class size of seven million makes achievement absurd
jokes.explanation: Exaggerated class size makes academic achievement both impressive and ridiculous

jokes.text: within 100 years, computers will be twice as powerful... 10,000 times larger... and so expensive that only the five richest kings of Europe will own them.
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards prediction about computer development
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of failed technology predictions - computers getting larger and more expensive

jokes.text: the computer matches would be so perfect as to eliminate the thrill of romantic conquest.
jokes.punchline: Predicting perfect matches would ruin romance
jokes.explanation: Ironically prescient about modern dating apps reducing romantic challenge

jokes.text: 200,000 precisely ordered punch cards... comprising the world's very first tic-tac-toe program.
jokes.punchline: Massive effort for simple tic-tac-toe game
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between enormous effort and trivial result

jokes.text: Only the top human players could beat it.
jokes.punchline: Program so bad that humans can easily beat it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making the program even more pathetic than expected

jokes.text: You're one of the good ones.
jokes.punchline: Uses problematic 'good immigrant' language
jokes.explanation: Shows Marge's well-intentioned but problematic language about immigration

jokes.text: Here's my I.D. which confirms my 'adultivity.'
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'adultivity' instead of 'adulthood'
jokes.explanation: Bart's childish attempt to sound adult backfires with made-up word

jokes.text: Your parents were Herb and Judy Nahasapeemapetilon.
jokes.setup: Creating fake American identity for Apu
jokes.punchline: Keeping impossible-to-pronounce Indian surname
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake identity that defeats its own purpose by keeping ethnic surname

jokes.text: The 'Ny' Mets are my favorite squadron.
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces team name and calls them 'squadron'
jokes.explanation: Apu's awkward attempt at American sports talk reveals his foreignness

jokes.text: I've got Tom Cruise and Nicole Kidman staring at me... from Entertainment Weekly with their dead eyes
jokes.setup: Apu replacing elephant statue with American celebrities
jokes.punchline: Describes magazine photos as having 'dead eyes'
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about celebrity worship vs. spiritual imagery - 'dead eyes' is haunting

jokes.text: A cheap, $2,000 forgery.
jokes.punchline: Calling $2,000 forgery 'cheap'
jokes.explanation: Ironic use of 'cheap' for expensive forgery shows Apu's desperation

jokes.text: You must love this country more than I love a cold beer on a hot Christmas morning.
jokes.punchline: Weird comparison to cold beer on Christmas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer sentiment - sincere but expressed through bizarre beer analogy

jokes.text: I'd rather eat poison. My name's already Selma Bouvier Terwilliger Hutz McClure.
jokes.setup: Asked to marry Apu for citizenship
jokes.punchline: Lists her multiple married names showing marriage fatigue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma character moment - her marriage history creates the punchline

jokes.text: God knows it's long enough without Nahassapetapeeta-whatever.
jokes.punchline: Can't even pronounce Apu's name correctly
jokes.explanation: Selma's casual dismissal and mispronunciation shows her attitude

jokes.text: Hey! The government don't control the sky. What if you lived in a balloon?
jokes.setup: Looking for ways to help Apu avoid deportation
jokes.punchline: Suggests living in a balloon to escape jurisdiction
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike solution to complex legal problem

jokes.text: That's it! Did you hear that, Mom? She's as dumb as me.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks Lisa is being dumb like him
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness about his intelligence while missing Lisa's actual insight

jokes.text: But why does the American flag have precisely 47 stars?
jokes.setup: Homer testing Apu on flag knowledge
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't know the correct number of stars
jokes.explanation: Homer as incompetent tutor - doesn't know basic facts he's testing

jokes.text: The library must have purchased it during the brief period in 1912... after New Mexico became a state, but before Arizona did.
jokes.setup: Explaining why flag has wrong number of stars
jokes.punchline: Apu knows more specific American history than Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - immigrant knows more detailed American history than native-born tutor

jokes.text: No, no and no. Maybe we should start all over with the electrical college.
jokes.setup: Homer failing to teach government branches
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'electoral college' as 'electrical college'
jokes.explanation: Homer's malapropism shows his own ignorance while tutoring Apu

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Just say slavery.
jokes.setup: Apu giving complex answer about Civil War causes
jokes.punchline: Examiner wants simple one-word answer
jokes.explanation: Satire of oversimplified citizenship test answers vs. complex historical reality

jokes.text: Now, which way to the welfare office? What? I'm kidding. I'm kidding. I work. I work.
jokes.punchline: Jokes about welfare stereotype then clarifies he's kidding
jokes.explanation: Apu makes dark joke about immigration stereotypes then reassures everyone

jokes.text: Now, which way to the welfare office? What? I'm kidding. I'm kidding. I work. I work.
jokes.punchline: Joking about welfare stereotype, then clarifying
jokes.explanation: Dark humor addressing immigration stereotypes - Apu making joke then backing away

jokes.text: Except Native Americans. - Yeah. Native Americans like us. - No. I mean American Indians. - Like me.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining who true Americans are
jokes.punchline: Homer and Apu both misunderstand 'Native Americans'
jokes.explanation: Confusion over terminology creates circular misunderstanding between characters

jokes.text: If I could just say a few words... I'd be a better public speaker.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission about speaking ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge self-deprecation delivered with excellent comedic timing

jokes.text: Democracy doesn't work!
jokes.punchline: News anchor declares democracy a failure
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny political commentary delivered with news anchor authority

jokes.text: Oh! Today I am truly an American citizen. - Jury duty!
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately burdens him with jury duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of citizenship celebration with immediate civic duty reality

jokes.text: Ach! Ingrates.
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating while Willie presumably gets deported
jokes.punchline: Willie's bitter final word
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy tag - Willie gets deported while everyone celebrates Apu staying
